pattern: Each ship is counted exactly once by tallying only its top-left cell: an 'X' that has no 'X' immediately above or to its left, which needs a single pass and no marking.
hints: Every battleship has a unique leftmost-topmost cell; if you count only those you never double-count a ship.
A cell starts a new ship when it holds 'X' but the cells directly above and directly to its left are not 'X'.
Scan the grid once; increment the count when board[r][c] is 'X' and (r == 0 or board[r-1][c] != 'X') and (c == 0 or board[r][c-1] != 'X').

edge_inputs.description: one horizontal and one vertical ship
single occupied cell is one ship
separated single-cell ships in a row
single long horizontal ship counts once
single long vertical ship counts once
ships stacked with a required gap between them

stress.description: random valid boards: place non-adjacent 1xk and kx1 ships so the separation rule holds
stress.note: ships are placed only when the surrounding 8-neighborhood is clear, keeping boards valid
